title: cissus voinieriana
intro: Cissus voinieriana, also known as the air potato vine or kiokom, is a perennial climber native to tropical Africa. It is a type of plant that belongs to the family Vitaceae, which also includes grapes and kiwis. Cissus voinieriana has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to promote health benefits, including reducing arthritis symptoms, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting bone density.
